# Research Results

## 2026-03-28

### Databricks
- **[Azure Databricks Lakebase Reaches GA at FabCon 2026]** — Azure Databricks announced the general availability of Lakebase, a managed serverless Postgres service for AI agents, now available in 14 Azure regions. Adoption has grown at more than twice the rate of Databricks' data warehousing product. (Source: [Databricks Blog](https://www.databricks.com/blog/whats-new-azure-databricks-fabcon-2026-lakebase-lakeflow-and-genie), 2026-03-25)
- **[Agent Bricks Supervisor Agent Adds Long-Running Task Support]** — Agent Bricks Supervisor Agent added long-running task mode, automatically breaking complex tasks into multiple request/response cycles using task continuation to avoid timeouts. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/march), 2026-03-25)
- **[AI Runtime Brings GPU Support to Serverless Compute]** — AI Runtime, designed for deep learning workloads, now brings GPU support to Databricks serverless compute. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/march), 2026-03-25)
- **[Data Quality Monitoring Alerts Now in Beta]** — Users can now create and view alerts for anomaly detection directly in the Databricks Data Quality Monitoring UI. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/march), 2026-03-25)
- **[Databricks Excel Add-in in Public Preview]** — The new Databricks Excel Add-in allows users to connect Microsoft Excel to their workspace, import data from tables or Metric Views, execute SQL queries, and analyze data directly in Excel. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/march), 2026-03-25)
- **[Databricks Asset Bundles Renamed to Declarative Automation Bundles]** — Databricks Asset Bundles has been officially renamed to Declarative Automation Bundles, signaling the platform's broader automation direction. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/march), 2026-03-25)
- **[External Data Sources in AI/BI Chat]** — Users can now connect external data sources such as Google Drive or SharePoint to AI/BI Chat conversations, and connect custom MCP servers as additional data sources. (Source: [Databricks AI/BI Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/ai-bi/release-notes/2026), 2026-03-23)

### AI Innovation
- **[Anthropic "Claude Mythos" Model Revealed in Data Leak]** — A draft blog post found in an unsecured data store revealed Anthropic is testing a new model called "Claude Mythos," described as a "step change" in AI capabilities. The model reportedly achieves dramatically higher scores on coding, academic reasoning, and cybersecurity benchmarks compared to Claude Opus 4.6. (Source: [Fortune](https://fortune.com/2026/03/26/anthropic-says-testing-mythos-powerful-new-ai-model-after-data-leak-reveals-its-existence-step-change-in-capabilities/), 2026-03-26)
- **[Anthropic Wins Preliminary Injunction Against Pentagon]** — A federal judge in San Francisco granted Anthropic a preliminary injunction in its lawsuit against the Trump administration over the Defense Department's blacklisting of the company, citing "classic illegal First Amendment retaliation." (Source: [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/26/anthropic-pentagon-dod-claude-court-ruling.html), 2026-03-26)
- **[Claude Computer Use Launches for Pro and Max Users]** — Anthropic launched Claude Computer Use in Cowork and Claude Code for Pro and Max plan users, allowing Claude to open files, click through apps, navigate browsers, and fill in spreadsheets on the user's desktop with no setup required. (Source: [Releasebot](https://releasebot.io/updates/anthropic), 2026-03-25)
- **[OpenAI Ads Business Surpasses $100M Annualized Revenue]** — OpenAI's advertising pilot surpassed $100 million in annualized revenue just six weeks after launch, expanding to more than 600 advertisers with self-serve access planned for April. (Source: [Tech Startups](https://techstartups.com/2026/03/27/top-tech-news-today-march-27-2026/), 2026-03-27)
- **[NASA Mars Rover Completes First AI-Planned Drives Using Claude]** — NASA's Perseverance rover completed the first Mars drives ever planned by AI, using Anthropic's Claude vision-language models. Over two drives covering 456 meters, the AI replaced a complex planning task that human operators had performed manually for 28 years. (Source: [Crescendo AI News](https://www.crescendo.ai/news/latest-ai-news-and-updates), 2026-03-24)
- **[LiteLLM Supply Chain Attack Discovered]** — A malicious file was found in LiteLLM v1.82.8 on PyPI that harvested SSH keys, cloud credentials, and secrets, affecting a library with 97 million monthly downloads. (Source: [Promethean AI Weekly](https://www.promethean-ai.com/post/the-week-in-ai-news-march-22-27-2026), 2026-03-27)

### AI Governance
- **[European Parliament Approves Digital Omnibus Changes to EU AI Act]** — On March 26, the European Parliament approved amendments to the EU AI Act under the Digital Omnibus package, formally opening trilogue negotiations. The vote proposes delaying high-risk AI system obligations from August 2026 to December 2027 for standalone systems, citing unavailable technical standards. (Source: [Gaming Tech Law](https://www.gamingtechlaw.com/2026/03/eu-ai-act-update-parliament-approves-digital-omnibus-changes/), 2026-03-26)
- **[GAO Report: OMB Must Address Privacy Gaps in Federal AI Guidance]** — The GAO published a report finding that OMB's government-wide AI guidance fails to address key privacy risks from federal AI adoption, identifying workforce skills gaps and scalability challenges as entirely unaddressed. (Source: [U.S. GAO](https://www.gao.gov/products/gao-26-107681), 2026-03-26)
- **[AI Civil Rights Act Introduced in Congress]** — Senator Edward Markey and Representative Yvette Clarke introduced the Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act to regulate algorithmic discrimination in housing, hiring, lending, healthcare, and education, mandating pre-deployment evaluations and independent third-party bias audits. (Source: [Drata](https://drata.com/blog/artificial-intelligence-regulations-state-and-federal-ai-laws-2026), 2026-03-22)
- **[Texas HB 1709 Passes State House with AI Risk Assessment Requirements]** — Texas HB 1709 passed the Texas House, requiring organizations using AI in consequential decisions to conduct and document risk assessments before deployment. If signed, it takes effect September 1, 2026. (Source: [Fisher Phillips](https://www.fisherphillips.com/en/insights/insights/the-next-state-to-regulate-ai-will-be-texas), 2026-03-20)
- **[SS&C Technologies Publishes Enterprise AI Governance Framework for Agentic AI]** — SS&C Technologies published a framework calling on the industry to adopt open standards for agentic AI deployment in regulated environments, addressing portability, build-time auditability, runtime observability, and data sovereignty. (Source: [SS&C Technologies](https://investor.ssctech.com/news-and-events/news-details/2026/SSC-Advocates-for-Open-Governance-Standards-in-Enterprise-AI/default.aspx), 2026-03-19)

### Data Governance
- **[IAPP Updates U.S. State Breach Notification Resource]** — The IAPP published an updated comprehensive chart summarizing all U.S. state breach notification laws, reflecting the rapidly expanding patchwork of state-level requirements and narrower definitions of personal information focused on identity theft and financial fraud risks. (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/news/a/iapp-updates-its-us-state-breach-notice-resource), 2026-03-27)
- **[Stryker Data Breach Class Action Filed After March 2026 Cyberattack]** — Stryker faces a class action lawsuit alleging negligent cybersecurity after a March 11 breach by Handala, an Iran-linked hacking group, that compromised private information. (Source: [ClassAction.org](https://www.classaction.org/news/data-breach-lawsuit-alleges-stryker-failed-to-protect-private-info-from-march-2026-cyberattack), 2026-03-26)
- **[DORA First Mandatory Reporting Cycle Hits March 2026 Deadline]** — European financial entities face the first mandatory Register of Information reporting deadline under DORA, requiring them to map every digital vendor in their supply chain using xBRL-CSV format, with penalties of up to 2% of global turnover for non-compliance. (Source: [Kiteworks](https://www.kiteworks.com/regulatory-compliance/dora-eu-banks-2026-compliance/), 2026-03-28)
- **[Ataccama Adds Agentic Data Observability to ONE Platform]** — Ataccama expanded its platform with agentic data observability capabilities, using AI to automatically triage incidents and suggest fixes rather than simply detecting data quality issues. (Source: [IT Brief UK](https://itbrief.co.uk/story/ataccama-adds-ai-data-observability-to-one-platform), 2026-03-28)
- **[Connecticut AG Settles CTDPA Enforcement Action Against TicketNetwork]** — Connecticut's Attorney General settled an enforcement action against TicketNetwork for alleged violations of the Connecticut Data Privacy Act, representing one of the early enforcement actions under the state's comprehensive privacy law. (Source: [Davis Wright Tremaine](https://www.dwt.com/blogs/privacy--security-law-blog/2026/03/trust-issues-march-2026), 2026-03-28)
